module: Get started with Copilot for Data Warehouse

questions.question: Which capability lets users describe what they want in plain English and have Copilot produce T-SQL?

options.A: Natural language to SQL
options.B: Capacity autoscaling

questions.explanation: Among Copilot's features is natural language to SQL, which converts natural language prompts into T-SQL queries.

questions.question: Which keyboard key accepts a full Copilot code-completion suggestion?

options.A: Enter
options.B: Esc

questions.explanation: You can accept a Copilot completion suggestion by pressing Tab, or dismiss it by continuing to type.

questions.question: What minimum capacity SKU is required to use Copilot in Fabric's Data Warehouse?

options.A: Any trial SKU
options.B: F1 only
options.C: A paid SKU of F2 or higher, or a P SKU

questions.explanation: Paid SKUs (F2 or higher, or a P SKU) are required to use Copilot; trial SKUs aren't supported.

questions.question: Before using code completion, which warehouse setting must be verified as enabled?

options.A: Show Copilot completions
options.B: Enable result set caching

questions.explanation: Before you start, verify that the Show Copilot completions setting is enabled in the warehouse Settings under the Copilot pane.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key capability of the Copilot chat pane in Fabric's Data Warehouse?

options.A: Provisioning new Fabric capacities
options.B: Converting natural language prompts into T-SQL queries, explaining queries, and helping fix errors

questions.explanation: The chat pane can convert natural language to T-SQL, answer data warehousing questions, provide natural language explanations of T-SQL, and assist with resolving T-SQL errors.

questions.question: What does the /explain chat command do?

options.A: Generates a natural language response to a general prompt
options.B: Fixes errors in the active query
options.C: Provides an explanation for the query in the active query tab

questions.explanation: The /explain command provides an explanation for the query in the active query tab.

questions.question: Which TWO AI-powered quick actions are available at the top of the SQL query editor near the Run button?

options.A: Explain and Fix
options.B: Generate and Optimize

questions.explanation: Copilot provides two AI-powered quick actions, Explain and Fix, located at the top of the SQL query editor near the Run button.

questions.question: What must happen before the Fix quick action button becomes enabled?

options.A: You must highlight the entire query
options.B: You must run a query that returns an error
options.C: You must open the Copilot chat pane first
options.D: You must define schema relationships in the model view

questions.explanation: The Fix button is only enabled after you run a query that returns an error; Copilot automatically uses that error message as context, so no extra input is needed.

questions.question: A data engineer enables Copilot but no ghost-text suggestions appear while typing in the SQL editor. The capacity is F4 in a supported region and the tenant switch is on. What is the most likely setting to check?

options.A: Result set caching is disabled
options.B: The 'Show Copilot completions' option is turned off in the warehouse Settings Copilot pane
options.C: The warehouse has no defined primary keys
options.D: The query tab is in read-only mode

questions.explanation: Inline ghost-text completions depend on the 'Show Copilot completions' setting being enabled in the warehouse Settings under the Copilot pane; the status bar also indicates whether completions are active.

questions.question: An analyst is on a Fabric trial and wants to use Copilot in the warehouse, but the feature is unavailable. What is the cause?

options.A: Trial SKUs aren't supported; a paid F2+ or P SKU is required
options.B: Copilot only works in lakehouses, not warehouses
options.D: Copilot requires a Power BI Premium license per user

questions.explanation: Copilot requires paid SKUs (F2 or higher, or a P SKU); trial SKUs aren't supported, which explains why it is unavailable on a trial.

questions.question: A developer wants Copilot completions to produce a query summarizing trips by hour on non-holiday weekdays. What is the recommended way to steer the suggestion within the editor?

options.A: Rename the warehouse to match the intent
options.B: Type a -- comment describing the intent, e.g. -- distribution of trips by hour on working days (non-holiday weekdays)
options.C: Switch the editor to dark mode

questions.explanation: Leaving a descriptive -- comment about the query intent prompts Copilot code completion to generate a corresponding query.

questions.question: While reviewing a Copilot completion, a developer wants to see other valid ways Copilot would write the same query before accepting one. What should they do?

options.A: Press Esc repeatedly until a new query appears
options.B: Hover over the suggestion to preview the alternative suggestions Copilot offers
options.D: Delete the query and retype it identically

questions.explanation: Copilot can provide different suggestions for the same input; you can hover over the suggestion to preview the other options and select the best fit.

questions.question: A team member ran a query that failed, and now wants Copilot to repair it without manually pasting the error message. Which approach fits this directly?

options.A: Use the Explain quick action
options.B: Use the Fix quick action, which uses the SQL error message as context automatically

questions.explanation: The Fix quick action becomes enabled after a query returns an error and automatically uses that error message as context, so no extra input is needed.

questions.question: A new hire wants Copilot to annotate an existing complex view query with inline comments describing what each part does, without changing the logic. Which feature should they use?

options.A: The Fix quick action
options.B: The Explain quick action, after highlighting the query or section

questions.explanation: The Explain quick action adds a summary at the top and inline comments throughout to describe functionality; you highlight the query or portion first.

questions.question: A user opens the Copilot chat pane for the first time and is unsure how to begin. What does the chat pane provide to help them get familiar with Copilot?

options.A: A guided video tutorial
options.B: Starter prompts

questions.explanation: The chat pane provides starter prompts to help you get familiar with Copilot.

questions.question: A developer generates a query with one chat prompt, then sends a follow-up '/fix using CTAS instead of ALTER TABLE' without restating the original query. Why does this work?

options.A: Copilot stores all queries permanently in the warehouse metadata
options.B: The chat pane retains context across prompts within the same session, enabling multi-turn refinement
options.C: ALTER TABLE and CTAS are interchangeable keywords
options.D: The /fix command always regenerates from scratch

questions.explanation: The Copilot chat pane retains context across prompts within the same session, so follow-up messages can reference earlier queries for iterative refinement.

questions.question: A data engineer finds Copilot's generated JOINs are frequently incorrect across related tables. Which best practice most directly improves JOIN accuracy?

options.A: Disabling code completion
options.B: Establishing schema relationships in the model view of the warehouse

questions.explanation: Establishing schema relationships in the model view enhances the accuracy of JOIN statements, allowing Copilot to infer connections between tables effectively.

questions.question: An organization's tables use cryptic names like T1, C2, and X. Copilot frequently misinterprets prompts. According to best practices, what should they do?

options.A: Add more capacity
options.B: Use expressive, meaningful names for tables and columns to help Copilot understand the schema
options.C: Translate the schema into English column abbreviations

questions.explanation: Using expressive and meaningful names for tables and columns is crucial; descriptive names help Copilot understand the schema and improve generated query accuracy.

questions.question: A developer wants Copilot to better understand the intent of a multi-table query directly within the query they are building. Which technique is recommended?

options.A: Add comments at the top of the query using -- to guide Copilot on intent
options.B: Remove all foreign keys
options.D: Convert the query to a stored procedure

questions.explanation: Adding comments at the top of your query using -- can guide Copilot in understanding the query's intent, and comments help with complex multi-table queries.

questions.question: A user submits prompts in French and is frustrated by inaccurate T-SQL output. What is the most relevant best-practice explanation?

options.A: Copilot does not support JOINs in non-English schemas
options.B: Natural language to SQL currently supports English to T-SQL, so prompts should be in English
options.C: French prompts require a P SKU
options.D: Copilot only works with English column data

questions.explanation: Natural language to SQL currently supports English to T-SQL; prompts should be written in English to take full advantage of the feature.

questions.question: A data engineer asks Copilot chat 'How can I import data into my Fabric Warehouse?' What category of capability is being used?

options.A: Code completion ghost text
options.B: Answering questions about data warehousing concepts via the chat pane

questions.explanation: The chat pane lets you ask questions about your data warehouse or general data warehousing concepts and receive answers, such as how to import data.

questions.question: In the chat pane, a user runs '/fix' on a query that references HourNumber from the wrong table. Which describes Copilot's expected behavior?

options.A: It deletes the query entirely
options.B: It fixes the error, for example correcting the reference to HourNumber from the Time table

questions.explanation: The /fix command fixes errors in the active query; in the example Copilot automatically fixed the reference to HourNumber from the Time table.

questions.question: A team wants to confirm at a glance whether Copilot completions are currently active without opening Settings. Where can they look?

options.A: The workspace governance page
options.B: The status bar at the bottom of the query editor

questions.explanation: The status bar at the bottom of the query editor shows whether completions are active at a glance.

questions.question: Before using Copilot quick actions, what context sources does Copilot draw on to provide relevant feedback?

options.A: Only the highlighted text
options.B: Warehouse schema, query tab contents, and execution results

questions.explanation: Copilot uses your warehouse schema, query tab contents, and execution results to provide relevant feedback for quick actions.

questions.question: An administrator needs Copilot available to the data warehouse team but Copilot does not appear for anyone in the tenant. What is the first thing to verify?

options.A: That each user has a separate Copilot license file
options.B: That the administrator has enabled the Copilot tenant switch and the capacity is in a supported region
options.C: That result set caching is enabled
options.D: That all tables have meaningful names

questions.explanation: Prerequisites state the administrator must enable the Copilot tenant switch and the capacity must be in a supported region (plus a paid F2+/P SKU).

questions.question: A developer wants Copilot to explain only one CTE within a large query, not the entire script. What is the correct quick-action workflow?

options.A: Run the whole query first, then click Fix
options.B: Highlight just that section, then select the Explain button

questions.explanation: Before selecting Explain, you highlight the query or the portion you want explained; you can select the whole query or just a section.

questions.question: A workspace's tenant restricts data processing to specific geographic regions. The team plans to roll out Copilot. What must they confirm?

options.A: That all users are in the same time zone
options.B: That the necessary permissions for the geographic-region data processing restriction are enabled
options.C: That the warehouse uses only English column data
options.D: That trial SKUs are upgraded to A SKUs

questions.explanation: If tenant settings restrict data processing to specific geographic regions, you must confirm that the necessary permissions are enabled for Copilot to work.

questions.question: Contoso Logistics runs a Fabric F8 warehouse in a supported region with the Copilot tenant switch enabled. Their tables are named with descriptive names (Trip, Date, Payment) and schema relationships are defined in the model view. A junior analyst, working in English, wants to: (1) write queries faster with inline help, (2) understand an existing payment-analysis view, (3) repair a query that failed at runtime due to a wrong column reference, and (4) ask conceptual questions about importing data. Which combination of Copilot features correctly maps to these four needs, in order?

options.A: Code completion ghost text; Explain quick action; Fix quick action; chat pane question
options.B: Fix quick action; code completion; chat /generate-sql; Explain quick action
options.C: Chat /help; Fix quick action; code completion; Explain quick action
options.D: Explain quick action; chat pane question; code completion; Fix quick action

questions.explanation: Writing faster with inline help is code completion (ghost text accepted with Tab); understanding an existing view is the Explain quick action (highlight then Explain, adding inline comments and a summary); repairing a runtime-failed query is the Fix quick action (enabled after an error, using the error message as context); and asking conceptual questions like how to import data is the chat pane answering data warehousing concepts. Their descriptive names and defined schema relationships also align with best practices that improve generated-query and JOIN accuracy, and English prompts match the supported natural language to SQL direction.

questions.question: A developer types the comment '-- Get the number of trips performed by each medallion' above an empty line. Which T-SQL would Copilot code completion most appropriately suggest to satisfy this intent?

options.A: SELECT Medallion, COUNT(*) AS TripCount FROM dbo.Trip GROUP BY Medallion;
options.B: DELETE FROM dbo.Trip WHERE Medallion IS NULL;
options.D: ALTER TABLE dbo.Trip ADD Medallion INT;

questions.explanation: The comment expresses intent to count trips per medallion, so Copilot would generate an aggregation grouping by Medallion with COUNT(*). The other statements modify schema or delete data rather than answer the question.

questions.question:
Copilot's Explain quick action annotates this snippet:

SELECT PaymentType, COUNT(T.PaymentType) AS PaymentsCount, SUM(TotalAmount) AS TotalAmountProcessed
FROM dbo.Trip AS T
JOIN dbo.[Date] AS D ON T.[DateID] = D.[DateID]
WHERE YEAR(D.[Date]) = 2013
GROUP BY PaymentType;

Which inline comment best describes the WHERE clause?

options.A: Joins the Trip table to itself
options.B: Filters the results to include only records from the year 2013
options.C: Sums the total amount for each payment type

questions.explanation: The WHERE YEAR(D.[Date]) = 2013 clause filters to only records from the year 2013, matching the style of inline comment Copilot's Explain action produces.

questions.question: A query failed because it used ALTER TABLE in a way that was inefficient for a rebuild. In chat, the developer sends the follow-up '/fix using CTAS instead of ALTER TABLE'. What is Copilot expected to do?

options.A: Ignore the prior context and ask for the full query again
options.B: Use the retained session context to revise the earlier query to use CREATE TABLE AS SELECT (CTAS) instead of ALTER TABLE

questions.explanation: Because the chat pane retains context across prompts in a session, the /fix follow-up can reference the earlier query and revise it to use CTAS instead of ALTER TABLE without restating the full context.

questions.question: Given a well-modeled warehouse with a defined relationship between dbo.Trip(DateID) and dbo.[Date](DateID), a developer prompts Copilot in English: 'total trip amount by year'. Which generated query best leverages the defined schema relationship?

options.A: SELECT YEAR(D.[Date]) AS Yr, SUM(T.TotalAmount) AS TotalAmount FROM dbo.Trip AS T JOIN dbo.[Date] AS D ON T.DateID = D.DateID GROUP BY YEAR(D.[Date]);
options.B: SELECT SUM(TotalAmount) FROM dbo.Trip;

questions.explanation: A defined relationship on DateID lets Copilot infer the correct JOIN; grouping by YEAR(D.[Date]) with SUM(T.TotalAmount) produces total trip amount by year. The other options do not join or aggregate by year.

questions.question: A poorly worded comment '-- stuff for the thing by the other thing' yields a low-quality Copilot completion. Rewriting it as which comment best follows the prompting best practices?

options.A: -- distribution of trips by hour on non-holiday weekdays
options.B: -- query

questions.explanation: Best practices call for clear, concise descriptions that specify intent (columns, aggregations, filters). A comment naming the metric (distribution of trips), the grouping (by hour), and the filter (non-holiday weekdays) guides Copilot far better than vague text.

questions.question: A developer accepts a Copilot ghost-text suggestion that produced 'SELECT PaymentType, COUNT(*) ...' but only wants the next word committed, not the full line. Which action accomplishes accepting just the next word?

options.A: Press Tab
options.B: Press Ctrl+Right (Cmd+Right on macOS)

questions.explanation: Tab accepts the entire suggestion; to accept only the next word of a completion, use Ctrl+Right (Cmd+Right on macOS).

questions.question: After running a query, the engine returns: 'Invalid column name HourNumber'. The developer clicks the Fix quick action. Which outcome aligns with how Fix works?

options.A: Fix asks the developer to paste the error text manually before proceeding
options.B: Fix uses the returned error message as context and corrects the invalid reference, e.g. resolving HourNumber to the correct Time table column
options.C: Fix rewrites the query in a different programming language
options.D: Fix only works if completions are disabled

questions.explanation: The Fix quick action becomes enabled after a query returns an error and automatically uses that SQL error message as context to resolve syntax or logic errors, such as correcting the HourNumber reference, without extra input.
